zh.Ulcombe, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特武爾可姆
zh.Saltwood Castle, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特薩爾特武德
zh.of Ulcombe, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特武爾可姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Ralph de St Leger於1091年出生在英國素可費艾利特時，他的父親William de St Leger31歲，母親Cecilia Romney27歲。他於1120年之前與Miss de Crevequer在英國英格蘭肯特武爾可姆結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1155年在英國英格蘭肯特武爾可姆享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ralph de St Leger于1091年出生在英国素可費艾利特时，他的父亲William de St Leger31岁，母亲Cecilia Romney27岁。他于1120年之前与Miss de Crevequer在英国英格兰肯特武爾可姆结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1155年在英国英格兰肯特武爾可姆享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Southern French: variant of   St  .   Romain  .

variants.name: Romain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Alain, Altagrace, Aurore, Camille, Dominique, Edele, Georges, Henri, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Jean-Michel, Jeanmarie.
French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and English: variant of   Roman  .

nameMeanings.name: Leger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Lucien, Pierre, Yvon, Antoine, Aurele, Emile, Jacques, Marcel, Zoel, Adelard.

French (Léger) and English: from the ancient Germanic personal name Leudgari, composed of the elements leud ‘people’ and gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’ and Latinized as Leodegar(ius). The name was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, bishop of Autun, whose fame contributed to the popularity of the name in France. See also Ledger and St. Leger .
French (Léger): nickname from léger ‘light, superficial’.

variants.name: Lege
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphie, Emile, Gaston, Henri, Leonce, Minus, Numa, Pierre, Rodolph.
German: topographic name meaning ‘swamp’.
Altered form of French Léger (see Leger ).

variants.name: Legere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Armand, Normand, Aurele, Jacques, Adelard, Alban, Antoine, Desire, Eloi, Monique, Yvon.
French (Légère): nickname from légère, a feminine form of Léger ‘light, superficial’ (see Leger 2).
Altered form of French   Leger   1 or 2.

variants.name: Lougee
meanings: Channel Islands: altered form of Laugee, a variant of French Lauger, itself either a variant of Léger (see Leger ) or a variant, with fused definite article l', of Auger .

History: The first recorded American bearer of this surname was John Lougee from the isle of Jersey. Born Jean Laugee and by trade a knitter, he was aged c. 18 or 19 when he emigrated to the USA in the late 1600s, settling in Exeter, NH, where he became known as John Lougee, marrying Anne, the daughter of Colonel Moses Gilman.

variants.name: Ledger
meanings: English: from a Norman personal name, Leodegar, Old French Legier, of ancient Germanic origin (see Leger 1).
English: metonymic occupational name from Middle English legger ‘one who lays stone, a mason’, a derivative of Old English lecgan ‘to lay’.
Americanized form of French   Leger   1.

variants.name: St. Leger
meanings: French (Saint-Léger): habitational name from any of several places in various parts of France named Saint-Léger (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Leodegar, in French St. Léger, the martyred 7th-century bishop of Autun), or a nickname with the prefix Saint as an (ironic) attachment to the personal name or surname Léger (see Leger 1).

Haitian (also Saint-Léger): from Saint-Léger, a nickname, ornamental name, or personal name composed of the French prefix Saint and the personal name Léger (see Leger 1), or perhaps a habitational name from a place in Haiti called Saint-Léger (compare 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Saint-Léger in La Manche or Saint-Léger-aux-Bois in Seine-Maritime, France (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Ralph de St Leger was born in 1091, in Fairlight, Sussex, England, his father, William de St Leger, was 31 and his mother, Cecilia Romney, was 27. He married Miss de Crevequer before 1120, in Ulcombe, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1155, in Ulcombe, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ralph de St Leger est né en 1091, à Fairlight, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, William de St Leger, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Cecilia Romney, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Miss de Crevequer avant 1120, à Ulcombe, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en 1155, à Ulcombe, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ralph de St Leger 1091 in Fairlight, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de St Leger 31 und seine Mutter Cecilia Romney 27. Er hat vor 1120 in Ulcombe, Kent, England, United Kingdom Miss de Crevequer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb 1155 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Ulcombe, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph de St Leger è nato il nel 1091 nel Fairlight, Sussex, England, il suo padre, William de St Leger, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Cecilia Romney, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Miss de Crevequer prima del 1120, nel Ulcombe, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1155, nel Ulcombe, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Ralph de St Legerが1091年にイギリススセックスファイルリトで生まれた時，父William de St Legerは31歳，母Cecilia Romneyは27歳であった。1120年以前にイギリスイギリスケントウルコンベでMiss de Crevequerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1155年にイギリスイギリスケントウルコンベで64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ralph de St Leger(이)가 1091년 영국 수세스 파어라이트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de St Leger(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Cecilia Romney(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1120년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 울코브에서 Miss de Crevequer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1155년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 울코브에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph de St Leger nasceu em 1091, em Fairlight, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de St Leger, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Cecilia Romney, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Miss de Crevequer antes 1120, em Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1155, em Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ralph de St Leger родился в 1091 г. в местности феирлаит, суссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени William de St Leger было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Cecilia Romney, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Miss de Crevequer перед 1120 г. в местности улкомб, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в 1155 г. в местности улкомб, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ralph de St Leger nació en 1091, en Fairlight, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, William de St Leger, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Cecilia Romney, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Miss de Crevequer antes de 1120, en Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en 1155, en Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 64 años.
